description: A designed warning graphic combining a stock photo with a slogan panel. The top two-thirds is a photograph of an interview/meeting scene: a bearded man in a gray blazer over a white shirt, wearing glasses, sits at a wooden table gesturing with both hands as he speaks across to another person seen from behind (dark hair, dark jacket) who holds a pen over an open notebook, staging an 'interview.' The bottom third is a solid dark-navy panel containing the DHS circular seal, the 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark in gray, then a bold gold/yellow headline 'Tell the truth in your interview' and white subtext 'If you lie, you could be banned from the U.S. for life.' Serious, official government style with a threatening compliance message aimed at immigration applicants/interviewees. The propaganda technique uses an authoritative interview scene plus a severe lifetime-ban warning to deter fraud. Tweet describes the USCIS Denver office facilitating the arrest of a Mexican national wanted for assault, discovered via background checks.
notable_text: Tell the truth in your interview. If you lie, you could be banned from the U.S. for life.
